channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden's commutation in 'kids for cash' scandal angers some Pennsylvania families
item.description: A judge who helped orchestrate a scheme to send children to for-profit jails in exchange for kickbacks was among the 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted by President Joe Biden this week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz reflects on Democrats’ shortcomings, return to Minnesota as political ground shifts
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is back at the Capitol and reacclimating to life after his vice presidential bid. Politics Friday host Brian Bakst and senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson unpack an interview with Walz about three months on the national stage and what awaits next year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Dana Ferguson, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Drone experts rule out US government experiment, unsure of other New Jersey drone phenomenon theories
item.description: Drone experts have little idea what the dozens of drone sightings over New Jersey could be, but have ruled out the possibility that they might be the work of a classified government program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Climate justice group has deep ties to judges, experts involved in litigation amid claims of impartiality
item.description: A controversial judicial advocacy organization funded by left-wing nonprofits continues to work with judges and experts involved in climate change litigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new Texas GOP rule could alter the House speaker’s race — and ban some Republicans from appearing in primaries
item.description: Republicans who vote against party leaders’ preferred candidate could be barred from running in primaries. Lawsuits could follow.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Robert Downen, KPRC 2

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny, acquitted in NYC subway chokehold, will join Trump's suite at football game
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Daniel Penny, a military veteran who choked an agitated New York subway rider and was acquitted of homicide this week, has been invited by Vice President-elect JD Vance to join Donald Trump's suite at the Army-Navy football game on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Raskin demands FBI vetting of Trump nominees, details on Epshteyn
item.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.) on Friday made a series of demands of the Trump transition team, asking for assurances President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees will undergo FBI vetting while also requesting details about aide Boris Epshteyn’s alleged profiting from promoting those seeking new posts.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden tells military children at Marine Corps Reserve toy drive to remember that they are loved
item.description: Jill Biden has told children from military families who helped the White House donate toys for the annual Marine Corps Reserve Toys for Tots campaign to remember that they are loved.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: WV lawmaker arrested after allegedly threatening to kill entire region's delegation over caucus beef
item.description: West Virginia Del-elect Joseph de Soto allegedly threatened to kill Eastern Panhandle lawmakers and the House Speaker after being informed they would vote him out of the caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These are the Trump appointees who have faced legal troubles
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several people to his next administration who have faced legal troubles — including Trump himself, who was convicted in a New York hush money case during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' from fall on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: White House says to 'expect more' climate funding before President Biden leaves office
item.description: President Biden is seeking to focus on climate funding during his final weeks in office, before President-elect Trump potentially takes aim at his green energy agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pledge against 'forever wars' could be tested with Syria in hands of jihadist factions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's commitment to ending U.S. involvement in foreign conflicts could be tested in Syria after jihadist rebels succeeded in toppling the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem claims Trump wielding nuclear strike authority 'should terrify you' — then people point out the obvious
item.description: Sen. Ed Markey, D-Mass., claims people should be terrified that President-elect Trump will have nuclear strike authority, but others pushed back, noting that Trump already had that authority during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: NYC to fund guaranteed income program: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City will provide funding for a guaranteed income program to support soon-to-be mothers experiencing housing insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: NYC 'won't be a safe haven' for migrants who commit crimes, mayor says
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday said he and incoming Trump administration border czar Tom Homan “have the same desire” to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: Asked at a Chinese Foreign Ministry briefing on Thursday about Trump's invitation, spokesperson Mao Ning responded: “I have nothing to share at present.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House: No evidence of public safety threat from NJ drone sightings
item.description: The White House on Thursday said there is no evidence of a national security or public safety threat after reports of drone sightings in New Jersey, highlighting that the government is working to identify what the mysterious sightings are.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House approves mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The House passed the annual defense policy bill Wednesday, sending the mammoth measure to the Senate for consideration ahead of Congress’s end-of-month deadline.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers edge closer to deal on government funding
item.description: Lawmakers say they are edging closer to a deal to keep the government's lights on in nine days but are still trying to work out differences over disaster aid and potential add-ons to the bill. There’s some optimism in the Capitol that bill text could arrive as soon as this weekend as the Dec.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences for 1,500 people, the largest act of clemency in a day
item.description: The 1,500 people had been serving long prison sentences that would have been shorter under today's laws and practices. Three Minnesota women are among those to get their federal convictions pardoned.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith and Chandra Colvin, MPR News

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams to discuss immigration with incoming US border czar
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A high-stakes talk will be held on Thursday between New York City Mayor Eric Adams and President-elect Donald Trump's incoming border czar, Tom Homan.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Future of Democratic Party rest in hands of one of these candidates
item.description: While the average American may not know who the chair of the Democratic National Committee is, the role has a direct impact on their lives.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News
